stories.title: CPSC Warning — Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets Pose Strangulation and Fall Hazards

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: CPSC WARNING: Stop using Tuyedoqe travel bassinets now. Strangulation + fall risk. ~250 units sold on Amazon Apr–Oct 2025, ~$40. Cut the cover in half and dispose. The company has not agreed to recall. cpsc.gov #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: If you have a Tuyedoqe travel bassinet: remove the foam padding, cut the cover in half, and throw it away. The company has not offered any refund or fix. Report your product at cpsc.gov #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 8:00 AM MT

Safety alert for COS parents who may own a Tuyedoqe travel bassinet.

The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission is warning parents to immediately stop using these products. About 250 units were sold on Amazon from April through October 2025 for around $40. The bassinets violate federal infant sleep product standards in three ways: the restraints create a strangulation hazard, the sides are too low (fall risk), and there's no stand — so using them on a raised surface creates another fall hazard.

Here's the part that makes this especially important: the company, Tuyedoqe of China, has NOT agreed to a recall. They're not offering a refund, a fix, or any remedy. The CPSC issued a notice of violation and the company did nothing.

What to do right now: Stop using the bassinet. Remove the foam padding. Cut the cover in half. Take a photo and dispose of it. Report your product at cpsc.gov.

If you know a family with a young baby who shops for budget baby gear on Amazon, please share this. The price point — around $40 — puts these in a lot of households.

Have you seen this product? What do you think about companies that ignore CPSC notices?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 8:30 AM MT

CPSC WARNING: Stop using Tuyedoqe travel bassinets immediately. Strangulation and fall risks. Company has not agreed to any recall or remedy. Cut the cover in half and dispose. See cpsc.gov for full details.

article.headline: CPSC Warning: Stop Using Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets Immediately — Strangulation and Fall Risks

stories.title: Pikes Peak Camping and Boating Coming to Rosemont Reservoir

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text: Camping and boating on Pikes Peak are coming. CPW and Colorado Springs Utilities are opening Rosemont Reservoir at 9,600+ feet for families — campsites + non-motorized boating, target July 4. cpw.state.co.us #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 10:00 AM MT

We've been waiting for this one, COS.

Colorado Parks and Wildlife and Colorado Springs Utilities have finalized an agreement to open Rosemont Reservoir on Pikes Peak to public recreation — and families are going to love it. The target date is around July 4, 2026, timed to the combined Colorado 150th anniversary and U.S. 250th anniversary celebrations.

The reservoir sits at more than 9,600 feet of elevation, about 12 miles southwest of Colorado Springs. The plan includes basic campsites, day-use parking, ADA-accessible restrooms, and non-motorized boating — that means hand-launched kayaks, canoes, paddleboards, and electric watercraft.

Camping will require a parks pass or the Keep Colorado Wild pass (which comes on your vehicle registration) plus a camping fee. Reservations will work like other Colorado State Park sites.

CPW is managing construction and stewardship, and they're expecting to break ground soon. Once it's ready, you'll have a new mountain camping option that didn't exist before — at nearly 10,000 feet, right on Pikes Peak.

We'll keep you updated as opening details are confirmed. Are you excited about this one?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 10:30 AM MT

Camping and kayaking on Pikes Peak. Seriously. Rosemont Reservoir is opening to families later this summer — CPW and Colorado Springs Utilities are making it happen at 9,600 feet. We'll have all the details as they're confirmed.

article.headline: Camping and Boating Coming to Rosemont Reservoir on Pikes Peak — What COS Families Should Know

stories.title: Get Outdoors Day Is THIS SATURDAY — Free Family Adventure at Memorial Park

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: Get Outdoors Day is THIS Saturday, June 6 — Memorial Park, 9 AM–2 PM, free. Archery, paddleboarding, fly fishing, climbing wall. First 650 kids 15 and under get free fishing poles. ppora.org #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: More than 3,000 COS families are expected at Get Outdoors Day on Saturday. Show up by 9 AM if your kids want free fishing gear — 650 pole sets go fast. Memorial Park, June 6, 9 AM–2 PM. ppora.org #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 3:00 PM MT

If you've been looking for a reason to get outside this weekend, here it is: Get Outdoors Day is THIS Saturday at Memorial Park, and it's completely free.

The event runs June 6 from 9 AM to 2 PM at Prospect Lake in Memorial Park. Colorado Parks and Wildlife, COS Parks Recreation and Cultural Services, and the Pikes Peak Outdoor Recreation Alliance put this together every year, and they're expecting more than 3,000 people.

Here's what your family can try: archery, stand-up paddleboarding, fly fishing instruction, a climbing wall, Touch a Truck, and a dunk tank. There's also a passport to get stamped at different vendor stations — completed passports go into a drawing for outdoor gear.

The biggest tip: arrive early. The first 650 kids age 15 and under each get a free fishing pole, bait, and instructions to try fishing in Prospect Lake. Last year those went quickly.

You don't need to sign up or buy anything. Just show up with sunscreen and comfortable shoes. Details at ppora.org.

Which activity are your kids most excited about?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 3:30 PM MT

THIS Saturday: Get Outdoors Day at Memorial Park, 9 AM–2 PM, free. Archery, paddleboarding, fly fishing, climbing, Touch a Truck. First 650 kids 15 and under get free fishing poles. More than 3,000 families expected — arrive early. No registration needed.

stories.title: Screen Time Gets a New Look in 2026 — What COS Parents Need to Know This Summer

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text: 2-year-olds now average 129 min/day on screens — 2x the WHO limit. A new UCL study links high screen use to lower vocabulary scores. Summer is the hardest season for limits. The AAP now focuses on quality over time. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text: Summer screen time tips for COS families: no screens before 18 months, max 1 hour ages 2–5, device-free mealtimes and bedrooms. PPLD Summer Adventure and COS parks make screen breaks easy this summer. ppld.org/summer #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 12:00 PM MT

Now that school's out, screens are everywhere — and new research gives us good reason to pay attention.

A 2026 study from University College London found that the average two-year-old now spends 129 minutes per day on screens. That's more than double the World Health Organization's recommendation of one hour or less. And the numbers aren't just about time: toddlers with the highest screen use in the study scored lower on vocabulary assessments and showed more emotional and behavioral difficulties than peers with low screen exposure.

The American Academy of Pediatrics updated its approach in 2026 to focus more on content quality and context, not strict clock limits. But the core guidelines still hold: no screens at all before 18 months, and for ages 2 to 5, a maximum of one hour of high-quality content — ideally watched together with a parent.

Practically, this means: watching with your kids and talking about what you're seeing matters. Device-free bedrooms and mealtimes make a real difference. And summer is the hardest time to hold the line, because the days are longer and routines go out the window.

The good news: COS has plenty of ways to make screen breaks easy. PPLD Summer Adventure is running now (sign up at ppld.org/summer), pools and splash pads are open, and programs through COS PRCS are available at coloradosprings.gov/PRCS.

What works for your family during summer when it comes to screens?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 12:30 PM MT

The average 2-year-old is spending 129 minutes a day on screens — 2x the WHO recommendation. New research links heavy toddler screen use to lower vocabulary scores. Summer is peak risk season. The AAP says focus on quality, not just clock time. We've got the full guide.

article.headline: Screen Time in 2026: New Research and What It Means for COS Families This Summer

stories.title: D49 Board Votes on 2026-27 Budget at June 11 Meeting

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: D49 parents: the Board of Education votes on the 2026-27 budget at its June 11 meeting at Sand Creek High School. The budget includes the Student Success Center opening August 2026. Check d49.org for the agenda. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 5:00 PM MT

D49 families have a chance to show up before the district sets its budget for next school year.

The D49 Board of Education will consider adopting the fiscal year 2026-27 budget at its Regular Board meeting on Thursday, June 11 at Sand Creek High School. The budget is expected to include funding for the District 49 Student Success Center, which is scheduled to open at the start of the 2026-27 school year in the former Falcon Elementary of Technology building.

The Student Success Center will offer two preschool classrooms Monday through Thursday, Base49 before- and after-school care, PEAK programs, and specialized support services for the district's most complex learners. It's a significant new resource for D49 families, and the budget vote is what makes it happen.

If you've got questions about what's in the budget, or you want to weigh in on how your district is spending its money, showing up to board meetings is how that works. Check d49.org for the full agenda and meeting time before you head out.

Will you be attending the June 11 meeting?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 5:30 PM MT

D49 families: June 11, Sand Creek High School. The Board of Education votes on the 2026-27 budget — which includes the Student Success Center opening this August. Check d49.org for the agenda and time. Your voice matters in these decisions.
